Yazelix Next integration is tracked in `yazelix-next-dtg`. That Bead owns the user-visible Mars + `yzn config` outcome and the flake release transaction. This Ratconfig Bead owns only the reusable Ratconfig source/API/theme work.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: add no production or dev crates. Build in-house with existing model/editor/render structs, Ratatui Style/Color primitives, and serde_json::Value matching for committed field values. Rejected alternatives: host-specific Mars/Yazelix theme policy, arbitrary user palette customization, file watching, or a new theming crate. Packaging impact: no dependency, feature, rust-version, or runtime closure changes.
close_reason: Completed Ratconfig-owned model-driven theme switching and fresh-eyes follow-ups: added ConfigUiTheme, ConfigUiThemeSwitcher, serde_json::Value mappings, initial theme resolution from committed model data, semantic light/dark palette support, README API docs, and post-successful-write theme switching for SetField and UnsetField/default-reset writes. Fresh-eyes fix: completion now uses stable intent source_id/path instead of stale field_index so hosts can rebuild/reorder the model before calling finish_successful_set_field or finish_successful_unset_field; Yazelix Next yzn-config path-dependency caller now uses the explicit completion methods for both save and reset. Dependency decision: no new crates. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test theme, cargo test, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, git diff --check, jq -s empty .beads/issues.jsonl, cargo fmt --manifest-path ../yazelix-next/crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml -- --check, cargo test --manifest-path ../yazelix-next/crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml, and git -C ../yazelix-next diff --check.

title: Analyze staged Space edits with Enter-only persistence
description:
Problem:
Space currently toggles/cycles a field and persists immediately. That is fast, but it makes exploratory edits risky because a user cannot preview or stage a value before committing it.

Proposal to analyze:
Make Space change the in-memory/editing value only, and make Enter the explicit save/persist action. Escape/cancel should discard the staged value and leave the backing config untouched.

Questions:
- Should this apply to all field kinds or only boolean/choice fields where Space is natural?
- How should dirty/staged state be represented in ConfigUiModel and draw_config_ui?
- Does Enter save the focused field only, or all staged changes if the model later supports multi-field staging?
- What should host applications receive through ConfigUiIntent so they remain the owner of file IO?

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide whether Ratconfig should support staged field edits with Enter-only persistence.
- If accepted, define the minimal public model/intent contract without host-specific policy.
- Preserve host ownership of validation, file IO, and apply policy.
- Add focused checks for Space not emitting a persistence intent and Enter emitting the save intent for a staged value.
- Record any rejected alternatives in the bead before implementation.
notes: Implementation decision: accept staged direct-choice edits without adding a new public intent or host persistence API. Existing ConfigUiEditState is the staging state. Normal Space should enter/stage direct-choice edits, especially booleans, and return ConfigUiIntent::None; Enter while editing continues to emit SetField with source_id/path/value. Rejected alternatives: adding multi-field dirty state now, because there is no accepted multi-save contract; changing file IO/apply policy, because hosts own persistence; making every text field stage on Space, because Space is ordinary text input there.
close_reason: Completed: accepted staged direct-choice behavior for booleans using the existing ConfigUiEditState rather than adding a new public intent or host persistence API. Normal Space and Enter on a bool now stage the toggled value and return ConfigUiIntent::None; Enter while editing emits the existing SetField intent with source_id, path, and value; Esc cancels without touching host-owned persistence. Updated the bool control footer and README wording, and added reducer plus optional crossterm-runner coverage for Space not persisting and Enter saving the staged value. Rejected alternatives are recorded in notes: no multi-field dirty state, no host IO/apply policy changes, and no text-field Space staging because Space is text input there. Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings, git diff --check, and jq -s empty .beads/issues.jsonl.

title: Cycle config UI row selection vertically
description: Let vertical row navigation wrap within the visible rows: moving up from the first row selects the last visible row, and moving down from the last row selects the first visible row. Keep empty lists stable at row 0. Crate decision: std/in-house only; no dependency changes.
close_reason: Implemented vertical visible-row wraparound in ConfigUiApp movement: up from first selects last, down from last selects first, empty filtered views stay at row 0. Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test reducer_wraps_vertical_row_navigation, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, cargo test --no-default-features, and cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings.

title: Decide runner helper public API before release
description:
## Why

The optional crossterm runner recently added several public helper functions around key/event conversion and reducer dispatch. They are useful for tests and embedding, but because `src/lib.rs` re-exports the entire runner module when the feature is enabled, each helper becomes public API. If those helpers are not intended as a long-term API surface, trimming them before publication would reduce semver burden and code surface.

## Evidence

- `src/crossterm_runner.rs:45-76`: four public helpers expose key conversion and dispatch: `crossterm_key_to_config_ui_key`, `crossterm_event_to_config_ui_key`, `handle_crossterm_key`, and `handle_crossterm_event`
- `src/crossterm_runner.rs:79-106`: the actual host-facing runner entrypoints are `run_config_ui` and `run_config_ui_with_details`
- `src/lib.rs:20-21`: `pub use crossterm_runner::*` re-exports all public runner symbols when `crossterm-runner` is enabled
- `README.md:98-119`: the documented runner example imports and uses only `run_config_ui`, not the lower-level conversion or dispatch helpers

## Scope

In scope:
- Decide whether the lower-level runner helpers are intentionally public, `pub(crate)`, private, or should be exposed under a narrower module path
- If trimming is accepted, update tests to call private helpers from the module test scope or assert behavior through the remaining public entrypoints where practical
- Keep the documented `run_config_ui` and `run_config_ui_with_details` flow stable unless the maintainer explicitly chooses a breaking API cleanup
- Update README only if the public runner surface changes

Out of scope:
- Do not change editor reducer key semantics
- Do not change terminal initialization/restoration behavior
- Do not add a compatibility shim for a surface that has not been published
- Do not move host validation, file writes, or apply policy into Ratconfig

## Implementation Notes

1. First determine whether this crate version has been consumed externally in a way that makes the helper API effectively committed
2. If not committed, prefer the smallest public API that supports the README example and expected host embedding use
3. Keep `crossterm_key_to_config_ui_key` public only if direct key conversion is a deliberate integration contract; otherwise make conversion/dispatch helpers private or `pub(crate)`
4. Preserve tests for key conversion and ignored events even if the helpers become private
5. If the API is intentionally kept, add a note or docs sentence explaining the intended low-level embedding use so future agents do not rediscover this as accidental slop

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0
- `cargo test --features crossterm-runner` should exit 0 and include runner tests
- `cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings` should exit 0
- `cargo test` should exit 0 for the default feature set
- `git diff --check` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the helper API has already been published or consumed and no explicit breaking-change approval is available
- Stop if deleting helpers requires weakening key conversion tests
- Stop if the cleanup changes reducer behavior, terminal lifecycle behavior, or host callback error propagation
- Stop if the only possible implementation adds more wrapper code than it removes

## Maintenance Notes

This is not automatically isomorphic: reducing public API can be behavior-changing for downstream callers. Dependency decision before implementation: add no new crates. Build in-house by changing visibility/export boundaries only. Packaging impact: no dependency or runtime closure growth; potential semver/API impact must be called out explicitly before landing.
notes: Implementation decision: no new crates. Treat lower-level crossterm conversion/dispatch helpers as accidental public API because README documents only run_config_ui and run_config_ui_with_details, and the runner commits are still local/ahead of origin in this branch. Keep the test coverage by using private helpers from the module test scope. Preserve terminal lifecycle, reducer semantics, and host callback error propagation.
close_reason: Completed: trimmed accidental lower-level crossterm runner helper API before publication by making key/event conversion and event dispatch helpers private, deleting the now-unused handle_crossterm_key wrapper, and keeping the documented run_config_ui/run_config_ui_with_details entrypoints stable. Runner tests still cover key conversion, ignored release/non-key events, and dispatch through the private event helper. Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings, and git diff --check.

title: Use Space to stage and Enter to save booleans
description: Refine the staged boolean interaction chosen in ratconfig-gpj: normal-mode Space stages the opposite boolean value, Enter while editing emits SetField, and Esc cancels. Normal-mode Enter on a boolean must not toggle or open a no-op edit. Other fields, file actions, and host-owned persistence remain unchanged. This follows the maintainer's explicit UX decision that Space then Enter is clearer than double Enter.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Space on a normal-mode boolean stages the opposite value and emits no persistence intent.
- Enter on a normal-mode boolean leaves the value/edit state unchanged and gives concise control guidance.
- Enter while the boolean edit is staged emits the existing SetField intent; Esc discards it.
- Non-boolean fields and file-action activation retain their current Enter/Space behavior.
- Normal reducer, rendering/control text, and optional crossterm-runner tests defend the interaction.
- Ratconfig format, default/all-feature/no-default-feature tests, and clippy gates pass before publication.
- Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock and README dependency example declare 2.0.0, and the published release is tagged v2.0.0 before downstream consumers update.
notes:
Dependency decision before implementation:
- Production crates: unchanged.
- Dev-only crates: unchanged.
- In-house logic: one reducer guard and control wording using existing ConfigUiEditState/ConfigUiIntent; no new public type, state machine, or host API.
- Rejected alternatives: double-Enter staging, immediate Space persistence, a global save queue, or a compatibility option supporting both interactions.
- Packaging impact: the documented stable contract treats observable reducer changes as major, so release Ratconfig 2.0.0/v2.0.0. Then publish aligned cursor and Next revisions before updating main Yazelix's Cargo pin/Nix hash and rerunning the no-override package gate.

SemVer classification before implementation:
- This changes documented normal-mode reducer semantics from Enter-or-Space staging to Space-only staging.
- Ratconfig's version policy explicitly names observable reducer-semantic changes as major. Version 2.0.0 is therefore required even though the source diff is small.
close_reason: Completed and released: normal-mode Space stages the opposite boolean without persistence, normal-mode Enter leaves the value/edit state unchanged with guidance, staged Enter emits SetField, and Esc cancels; non-boolean and file-action behavior is preserved. Ratconfig metadata, lockfile, README dependency example, and release notes declare 2.0.0; commit 110e749 is pushed and tagged v2.0.0. Verified cargo fmt, default/all-feature/no-default-feature tests, all-target clippy in all three feature modes with -D warnings, and git diff --check.

title: Make Ratconfig's host model source-first
description:
## Why

Ratconfig added generic per-source metadata for multi-file hosts, but ConfigUiModel still requires legacy single-config and cursor-specific paths. This leaves two ownership models in the public API, makes every host populate stale fields, and causes unmapped tabs to display the legacy active-config header.

## Evidence

- `src/model.rs:13-20`: ConfigUiModel carries `active_config_path`, two cursor-specific paths, existence/owner/write flags, and the newer generic `sources` collection together.
- `src/render.rs:214-229`: selected-source metadata is preferred, but any unmapped tab falls back to the legacy active-config fields.
- `README.md:51-79`: the minimal generic host example must populate empty cursor paths and single-config metadata even though sources are the reusable abstraction.
- `../yazelix-next/crates/yzn-config/src/model.rs:103-160`: Nova supplies empty legacy cursor paths while separately describing every real document through ConfigUiSource.
- `../yazelix/rust_core/yazelix_core/src/config_ui/model_builder.rs:257-280`: Classic also supplies both legacy model metadata and generic sources.

## Architecture Outcome

ConfigUiModel is source-first: generic ConfigUiSource metadata is the sole owner of config-document identity, path, existence, ownership, and write mode. Tabs without a source use an explicit neutral header state rather than implicitly borrowing an unrelated root config. Cursor-specific paths disappear from Ratconfig.

## Scope

In scope:
- Simplify ConfigUiModel, selected_config_source, and header rendering around generic sources.
- Remove the legacy active-config and cursor-specific model fields in an intentional major Ratconfig release.
- Update Ratconfig examples and focused model/render tests.
- Migrate both current consumers, Classic Yazelix and Nova, in coordinated pinned-revision transactions.
- Measure Ratconfig plus consumer production LOC before and after; the new shape must reduce combined ownership.

Out of scope:
- No schema loading, validation, config discovery, file IO, atomic writes, migrations, editor launching, or apply policy in Ratconfig.
- No Yazelix, Nova, Mars, Zellij, Yazi, cursor, Home Manager, or command-name policy in Ratconfig types.
- No compatibility shim that keeps both source metadata and the legacy model fields indefinitely.
- No redesign of tabs, diagnostics, file actions, or the config editor.

## Implementation Notes

1. Inventory all ConfigUiModel construction sites and any external published consumers before changing the public contract.
2. Define explicit rendering behavior for source-backed tabs and source-less operational tabs.
3. Delete the legacy fields and fallback branch rather than adding a third document abstraction.
4. Update Ratconfig tests and README to demonstrate a neutral non-Yazelix multi-source host.
5. Publish the Ratconfig major revision, then update Classic and Nova pins and construction sites.
6. Record the before/after production LOC across Ratconfig and both hosts.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test` should exit 0 with focused source-backed and source-less header tests.
- `cargo test --all-features` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --no-default-features` should exit 0.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` should exit 0.
- `rg -n "active_config_path|cursor_config_path|default_cursor_config_path|active_config_exists|config_owner|config_read_only" src README.md` should find no retained legacy public model ownership.
- Classic and Nova focused config-UI tests should pass against pushed Ratconfig revisions without local dependency overrides.
- The recorded combined production LOC should decrease.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the change starts moving host persistence, schema, or runtime policy into Ratconfig.
- Stop if source-less tabs cannot be represented neutrally without a broader UI redesign.
- Stop if a compatibility layer would leave both old and new ownership models live.
- Stop if current external consumers make a major release unacceptable until the maintainer chooses a migration policy.
- Stop if combined production LOC does not decrease.

## Maintenance Notes

- Priority P3: this is post-convergence API cleanup, not a cursor-work dependency or Nova cutover gate.
- Effort M; implementation risk MED; confidence HIGH.
- Dependency decision: add no production or dev crates; use existing model and renderer primitives.
- SemVer: removing public ConfigUiModel fields is a major change. Batch it with other accepted host-API cleanup instead of creating repeated major releases.
- Rejected alternative: retain the legacy fields as permanent fallback metadata. That preserves the duplicate ownership this bead exists to remove.
notes: Implementation checkpoint 2026-07-12: Ratconfig 3.0.0 removes the six legacy ConfigUiModel document fields, ConfigUiSource is the only model owner, and source-less tabs render neutral not-file-backed metadata. Classic routes edit paths through ConfigUiSource and resolves packaged cursor defaults/apply inputs from its host request. Nova drops the six stale constructor fields. Scoped production LOC across Ratconfig model/render, Classic model-builder/app/apply adapter, and Nova model fell from 6124 to 6103 (-21). Verified Ratconfig fmt, default/all-features/no-default-features tests, clippy -D warnings, zero legacy refs in Ratconfig src/README, Nova yzn-config 38 tests, Classic 35 config_ui tests plus the Home Manager integration test through a temporary local Ratconfig override. Remaining release transaction: manual approval, publish Ratconfig, update Classic's pinned git revision, rerun Classic without overrides, and commit consumer integrations. Concurrent Nova work owns unrelated yzn-config/Beads changes; preserve it.

title: Remove Yazelix schema extension hard-code from ratconfig
description:
## Why

Ratconfig is now a project-agnostic child crate, but `src/model.rs` still hard-codes Yazelix's JSON Schema extension key `x-yazelix` when extracting tab order. That keeps a Yazelix-specific schema namespace inside the reusable crate and violates the local boundary that host applications own product-specific schema metadata.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:12`: ratconfig owns project-agnostic Ratatui config editor primitives.
- `AGENTS.md:14`: Yazelix-specific Home Manager, Zellij, Yazi, runtime-refresh, and command-name policy stay out of this crate.
- `README.md:5`: README says Ratconfig is project-agnostic and hosts provide their own config schema and behavior.
- `src/model.rs:317`: `schema_tabs` extracts tabs directly from the schema.
- `src/model.rs:319`: `schema_tabs` hard-codes `.get("x-yazelix")`.
- `src/model.rs:642`: the unit test describes the behavior as reusable, but the fixture uses `"x-yazelix"`.
- Main Yazelix currently calls `schema_tabs(&schema, DEFAULT_TABS)` from `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/config_ui/model_builder.rs:18`, so changing this API requires a main integration update.

## Scope

In scope:
- Remove hard-coded knowledge of `x-yazelix` from ratconfig.
- Either make the schema extension key host-supplied, such as `schema_tabs(schema, extension_key, default_tabs)`, or move Yazelix-specific tab extraction fully into main Yazelix.
- Keep generic fallback behavior and the automatic `advanced` tab policy if it is truly generic; otherwise make that host-supplied too.
- Update ratconfig tests to use a neutral extension key or host-supplied key.
- Coordinate the main Yazelix consumer update in the same release transaction if the public API changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not rename Yazelix's existing `x-yazelix` schema metadata in the main repo unless a main integration bead explicitly decides to.
- Do not move Yazelix config schema, Home Manager ownership, validation, file IO, or apply policy into ratconfig.
- Do not do a broad config UI redesign.

## Implementation Notes

1. Decide whether `advanced` is a generic ratconfig convention or a Yazelix UI convention.
2. If keeping tab extraction in ratconfig, change the API so the host supplies the schema extension key and tests prove a non-Yazelix key works.
3. If moving extraction to main, delete `schema_tabs` from ratconfig and implement the `x-yazelix` read in main Yazelix's config UI adapter.
4. Publish the ratconfig child commit before updating main Yazelix's git dependency.
5. Update main Yazelix to consume the new API or local extraction path and run the coupled release transaction without local overrides.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should pass in ratconfig.
- `cargo test` should pass in ratconfig.
- A targeted grep in ratconfig, `rg -n "x-yazelix|Yazelix" src`, should show no source-owned hard-coded Yazelix schema extension references except deliberately generic non-Yazelix test names.
- Main Yazelix focused config UI tests should pass after the dependency update.
- Main Yazelix's Cargo lock should point at the pushed ratconfig commit, not a local override.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the main Yazelix consumer cannot be updated in the same cross-repo transaction after a ratconfig API change.
- Stop if the change starts moving Yazelix-owned schema semantics into ratconfig instead of removing them.

## Maintenance Notes

This is the only concrete extraction leftover found in the ratconfig source audit. Exact old crate/repo names (`yazelix-ratconfig`, `yazelix_ratconfig`, old GitHub URL) were already absent from ratconfig, yazelix-cursors, and main tracked source surfaces.

Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test, rg -n 'x-yazelix|schema_tabs\(' src, and a main Yazelix local override smoke test for cargo test -p yazelix_core config_ui.

title: Improve Ratconfig light-mode contrast
description:
## Why

Several Ratconfig elements are difficult to read in light appearance because foreground, border, status, and selection colors do not consistently contrast with the light background. The config UI should remain legible without users having to switch Mars back to dark mode.

## Evidence

- Runtime dogfooding report on 2026-07-14: some config-popup elements often have poor contrast in light mode.
- `src/render.rs:34-59` defines one `ConfigUiThemePalette`, but the light palette still uses named terminal colors such as `Color::Gray` for borders.
- `src/render.rs:1088-1121` remaps only a fixed set of named colors; every unmatched color passes through unchanged, while many renderer call sites still use hard-coded named colors.
- `src/render.rs:1656-1667` only proves that plain text and the selected background are not white. It does not defend readable semantic roles or selected-row combinations.

## Scope

### In scope

- Reproduce and list the concrete low-contrast elements in Ratconfig under Mars light appearance, including normal rows, selected rows, borders, metadata, status/error text, and detail/help text.
- Keep Ratconfig's centralized light/dark palette as the single owner and route the failing elements through semantic palette roles.
- Choose light-mode colors that remain distinguishable on both the panel background and selection background.
- Preserve dark-mode behavior unless a shared semantic-role correction is required.
- Publish the Ratconfig change; Yazelix owns its downstream dependency pin, README LOC scorecard, CHANGELOG, and integration checks.

### Out of scope

- A user-configurable theme system, Mars palette changes, terminal-wide color remapping, product-specific branches in Ratconfig, or a duplicate palette in Yazelix.
- Claiming universal WCAG compliance from raw RGB values alone; terminal rendering and transparency still require dogfooding.
- Adding a color/contrast dependency for a small fixed palette.

## Implementation Notes

1. Capture the failing semantic roles before changing colors.
2. Extend or simplify `ConfigUiThemePalette` and `themed_style` so renderer call sites express roles rather than scattered light-mode exceptions.
3. Prefer deleting hard-coded role colors over adding per-widget overrides.
4. Add one focused renderer contract that exercises representative normal, selected, status, and detail roles in light appearance. Assert meaningful separation/readability, not a mirror of every literal RGB value.
5. Keep the generic Ratconfig boundary: no Yazelix tab names, paths, or product policy in the child.

## Verification

- In Ratconfig: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` and `cargo test` pass.
- Focused renderer coverage fails on the current low-contrast role combinations and passes after the palette correction.
- Manual Mars dogfooding in light appearance confirms readable normal rows, selected rows, borders, metadata, errors/status, and details at the config popup's normal transparency.
- After publishing and locking Ratconfig in Yazelix: `nix flake check`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary-profile install pass.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop and capture a screenshot plus terminal color data if the problem is caused by Mars compositing rather than Ratconfig's emitted styles; move that failing layer to the correct owner.
- Stop if the fix requires a second theme/config surface or product-specific logic in Ratconfig.
- Stop if improved light contrast materially regresses dark appearance; separate the semantic roles before proceeding.

## Maintenance Notes

Owner: Ratconfig's renderer palette; Yazelix only consumes a published revision.

Effort: S/M. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

Rejected alternatives: scattered per-widget light-mode overrides, changing Mars globally, duplicating the palette in Yazelix, and preserving exact RGB literals with mirror tests.
notes: Transferred from yazelix-5vl-mq4 on 2026-07-14 because the renderer palette is owned by Ratconfig. The Yazelix issue is closed as a cross-repo duplicate. Ratconfig owns implementation and publication; Yazelix owns its downstream dependency pin and product integration.

title: Fix missing Rust test lane declarations
description:
## Why

Shared Yazelix agent rules require every first-party Rust file that contains `#[test]` to declare `// Test lane: ...` with an allowed lane. Several ratconfig files contain tests but lack the lane declaration, which makes the test ownership policy inconsistent and easy to miss during future edits.

## Evidence

- `src/jsonc.rs:210`: contains `mod tests` and `src/jsonc.rs:215` contains `#[test]`, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/migration.rs:162`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/toml_adapter.rs:333`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/contract/mod.rs:518`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/contract/toml.rs:148`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/model.rs`, `src/editor.rs`, and `src/render.rs` already demonstrate the expected `// Test lane: default` file-level pattern

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the appropriate `// Test lane: default` declaration to first-party Rust files that contain governed `#[test]` functions and currently lack it
- Keep the change mechanical unless a file genuinely belongs to a different allowed lane

Out of scope:
- Do not change test behavior, assertions, fixtures, public APIs, or formatting beyond the lane declarations
- Do not add new test coverage just to touch these files
- Do not add or change dependencies

## Implementation Notes

1. Re-run `rg -n "Test lane|#\\[test\\]|mod tests" src` and confirm the current set of files with governed tests.
2. Add `// Test lane: default` near the top of each missing first-party Rust file, matching the existing style in `src/model.rs`, `src/editor.rs`, and `src/render.rs`.
3. If a tested file clearly belongs in `maintainer`, `sweep`, or `manual` instead, document that reason in the implementation notes before choosing the non-default lane.

## Verification

- `rg -n "Test lane|#\\[test\\]|mod tests" src` should show every first-party Rust file with `#[test]` has a lane declaration
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0
- `cargo test` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the test lane policy has been changed or removed from the applicable `AGENTS.md` before implementation.
- Stop if the missing-lane list differs materially from the evidence and the correct lane is unclear.
- Stop if implementation appears to require behavior changes outside mechanical lane declarations.

## Maintenance Notes

- Dependency decision: no new crates; this is a mechanical policy-compliance cleanup using existing source comments only.
- Review focus: verify the lane declaration is present in every first-party Rust file with governed tests, not only the five cited examples.

title: Add numbered tab shortcuts
description:
## Why

Cycling tabs with Tab, Shift-Tab, h, or l is predictable but slow when users repeatedly move between non-adjacent tabs. Visible numeric shortcuts would make direct navigation discoverable and reduce repeated key presses without removing the existing controls.

## Evidence

- src/editor.rs:115-128: ConfigUiApp supports only next_tab and previous_tab cycling, both of which reset selected_row to zero
- src/editor.rs:364-396: normal-mode key reduction handles cyclic tab navigation but ignores digit keys
- src/render.rs:292-305: render_tabs displays host tab labels without shortcut hints
- src/crossterm_runner.rs:45-66: unmodified character keys already map to ConfigUiKey::Char, so digit shortcuts need no new terminal abstraction

## User-Visible Contract

Render the first nine tabs as (1) label through (9) label. In normal mode, pressing 1 through 9 selects the corresponding zero-based tab position and resets row selection to the first real row. Out-of-range digits do nothing. Search and edit modes keep digits as text input. Existing Tab, Shift-Tab, h, l, arrow, and host tab behavior remain unchanged.

If a model contains more than nine tabs, only the first nine receive numeric labels and direct shortcuts; later tabs remain visible with their original labels and reachable through cyclic navigation.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add presentation-only numbering in src/render.rs without changing ConfigUiModel::tabs values or host tab identity
- Add normal-mode 1 through 9 direct selection in src/editor.rs
- Preserve search-mode and edit-mode digit entry
- Preserve existing cyclic tab navigation and selected-row reset behavior
- Add focused editor, renderer, and optional crossterm-runner regression tests
- Document numbered navigation in README.md if the public control description needs it

Out of scope:
- No multi-digit shortcuts, 0 shortcut, key chords, configurable shortcut maps, or shortcuts beyond the ninth tab
- No host-specific tab names, ordering policy, schema behavior, persistence, or runtime policy
- No public model field, intent variant, dependency, or terminal-event abstraction
- No redesign of tab styling, layout height, or overflow behavior beyond the compact numeric prefix

## Implementation Notes

1. Derive displayed numbers from tab position at render time so host-owned labels and stable tab identifiers remain untouched
2. Handle digits only after ConfigUiApp has already routed active edit and search input, ensuring numeric values and queries still work
3. Validate the requested position against model.tabs before changing selected_tab or selected_row
4. Keep the shortcut logic small and internal unless a reusable public selection API is independently justified
5. Use the existing theme and tab highlight styles for numbered labels

## Verification

- cargo fmt --all -- --check should exit 0
- cargo test should exit 0 with reducer tests for direct selection, row reset, out-of-range digits, and preserved search/edit digit input
- cargo test --all-features should exit 0 with crossterm dispatch coverage for a digit shortcut
- cargo test --no-default-features should exit 0 and retain reducer coverage without rendering
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings should exit 0
- git diff --check should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if implementation requires mutating host-provided tab labels or using display text as tab identity
- Stop if digit shortcuts leak into active search or edit input handling
- Stop if supporting more than nine direct shortcuts expands into a chord or keymap subsystem
- Stop if compact prefixes cause tab rendering to require a broader overflow redesign

## Maintenance Notes

- Priority P3: this is low-risk, reusable navigation polish rather than a blocker
- Dependency decision: add no production or dev crates; use existing reducer, Ratatui Tabs rendering, and crossterm character conversion
- Packaging impact: publish a new Ratconfig revision and let downstream pinned-git hosts consume it through their normal release transaction
- Rejected alternative: alter ConfigUiModel::tabs to include numbers, because presentation metadata must not replace stable host labels
- Rejected alternative: use 0 for a tenth tab, because 1 through 9 is easier to discover and avoids a special-case mental model
close_reason: Implemented numbered labels and normal-mode 1-9 tab selection with row reset, mode-isolation regressions, crossterm dispatch coverage, and README documentation. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test, cargo test --all-features, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, and git diff --check.

title: Add ordered string-list editing
description:
## Why

Ratconfig currently treats allowed `string_list` multi-choice editing like a set. That works for unordered feature toggles, but it cannot represent host settings where the order of selected ids is meaningful. The implementation should add ordered-list controls without changing the existing unordered multi-choice contract by default.

## Evidence

- `src/editor.rs:539`: `edit_mode_for_field` maps any enum-backed `string_list` field to `ConfigUiEditMode::MultiChoice`.
- `src/editor.rs:723`: `multi_choice_status_value` reports enabled count and highlighted allowed value, not selected-item order.
- `src/editor.rs:738`: `toggled_string_list_input` toggles the highlighted allowed value in or out of the parsed list.
- `src/editor.rs:753`: toggled values are always passed through `ordered_string_list_values`.
- `src/editor.rs:758`: `ordered_string_list_values` rebuilds selected values in `allowed_values` order through a set, so arbitrary user order is lost.
- `src/editor.rs:787`: `is_enum_string_list_field` is only `kind == "string_list" && !allowed_values.is_empty()`, so there is no separate ordered-list behavior flag today.

## Scope

In scope:
- `src/editor.rs` edit behavior/mode changes for an explicitly ordered string-list field.
- `src/model.rs` only for a narrow behavior flag/variant if needed.
- `src/render.rs` only if the UI needs to display ordered selected values or reorder hints.
- Focused tests for preserving order, toggling, and reordering.

Out of scope:
- Do not change default `MultiChoice` set semantics for existing fields.
- Do not encode host/product ids, widget names, labels, file paths, apply behavior, or runtime refresh commands.
- Do not add drag/drop, mouse support, or a new UI framework.
- Do not make unordered scalar enums or boolean choice editing depend on this feature.

## Implementation Notes

1. Depend on the string-list helper bead so hosts can opt into ordered behavior through a small public API instead of relying on `kind` alone.
2. Add an explicit opt-in for ordered string-list editing, for example a `ConfigUiEditBehavior` variant or similarly small flag. Existing `string_list` fields should keep current allowed-values ordering.
3. Preserve parsed config order for selected values. Adding a new allowed value may append it to the selected list; removing a value should keep the order of the remaining values.
4. Add minimal reorder commands/handlers only where the existing editor architecture already routes choice actions. Avoid broad keymap or rendering rewrites.
5. Make invalid inputs fail through the same allowed-value validation path as current string-list parsing.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test` should exit 0, including tests that an ordered string-list field preserves `["b", "a"]` rather than normalizing it to allowed-values order, and that the default unordered multi-choice behavior remains unchanged.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if adding ordered controls requires a broad editor event-loop redesign rather than a narrow behavior extension.
- Stop if this starts changing existing unordered multi-choice behavior without an explicit opt-in.
- Stop if the implementation requires host-specific product concepts.
- Stop if verification fails twice for the same reason after a reasonable fix attempt.

## Maintenance Notes

- This is intentionally separate from the helper/API bead. If a host only needs config-order validation and JSON/TOML persistence, it should be able to use that bead without adopting ordered-list UI controls.
- Rejected alternative: reusing `allowed_values` order as the only ordering model. That keeps code small but fails settings where selected order is observable behavior.
notes: Fresh-eyes correction: Ctrl+j was replaced with uppercase J and Ctrl+k with uppercase K for ordered string-list reordering because Ctrl+J is commonly encoded as line feed/Enter by terminals. The public behavior is J/K reorder; no crate or packaging impact.
close_reason: Completed: added ConfigUiEditBehavior::OrderedStringList as the explicit opt-in for order-preserving string-list multiselect editing. Ordered fields keep parsed config order when toggling, append newly enabled values, remove disabled values without reordering survivors, and support uppercase J/K to move the highlighted enabled id down/up. Default string-list multiselect still canonicalizes to allowed_values order. Rendering shows ordered selected values and the J/K reorder hint for ordered fields only. Fresh-eyes fix replaced Ctrl+j/k because Ctrl+J is commonly encoded as line feed/Enter in terminals. No new crates or packaging impact. Verified with focused ordered/default string-list tests, crossterm Shift+J conversion coverage, cargo fmt --all -- --check, git diff --check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, cargo test --no-default-features, and cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings.

title: Render selected config source metadata
description:
## Why

The current header assumes one active config path for the whole UI. Multi-file hosts need the selected tab/source to show the relevant path, owner, existence, and write mode so users can tell which managed file they are editing. Without this, a UI with separate file-backed tabs can edit the right file internally but still display misleading global metadata.

## Evidence

- `src/model.rs:10`: `ConfigUiModel` has one `active_config_path`, `active_config_exists`, `config_owner`, and `config_read_only` for the whole UI
- `src/render.rs:75`: `render_header` reads only the model-level owner/write state/path
- `src/render.rs:82`: missing-file text is derived from the single `active_config_path`
- `src/render.rs:187`: tabs are rendered from bare `String` labels, with no source metadata
- `src/model.rs:39`: row filtering is tab-label based, while only the hardcoded `advanced` tab gets non-field operational rows

## Scope

In scope:
- Add project-agnostic source/tab metadata that can describe a host-owned config document: id, display label, path, exists/missing state, owner, and read-only/write mode
- Render header metadata from the selected tab/source when present, with the existing single-model path as a compatibility fallback
- Keep the `advanced` operational tab behavior intact unless a smaller replacement falls out naturally from the new model
- Add focused render/model tests for two file-backed tabs with different paths and write modes
- Update affected README examples after the API shape is settled

Out of scope:
- Do not load, create, patch, migrate, or atomically write any config file inside Ratconfig
- Do not include Yazelix-specific path names, Mars/Zellij wording, or Zellij sidecar policy in Ratconfig docs or model types
- Do not add status rows for product-specific native config surfaces in this bead
- Do not change text adapter semantics for JSONC or TOML

## Implementation Notes

1. Build on `ratconfig-cvf` so field source identity and UI source metadata share one id vocabulary
2. Prefer a small neutral type such as `ConfigUiSource`/`ConfigUiTabSource`; avoid a broad document-management abstraction
3. Keep old single-file construction reasonably ergonomic, either through fallback model fields or a tiny helper
4. Ensure narrow terminal widths still truncate the selected path cleanly and keep owner/mode/diagnostic metadata readable
5. Keep tests focused on the contract: selected source metadata, fallback behavior, and no regressions to advanced rows

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0
- `cargo test` should exit 0, including model/render tests for selected source metadata
- `cargo test --features crossterm-runner` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the implementation starts making Ratconfig responsible for host persistence or config discovery
- Stop if the API requires product-specific source kinds instead of neutral host-supplied metadata
- Stop if the header/layout change makes narrow-width rendering less stable than the current global metadata line

## Maintenance Notes

Dependency decision: add no crates. This should be model/rendering plumbing over existing `PathBuf`, owner enum, and render helpers. Rejected alternative: keep one global `active_config_path` and ask hosts to put the selected file path in field descriptions; that hides core context in the details pane and makes the header misleading for multi-file editors.
notes: User clarification: selected-source metadata is UI context only. This bead should not introduce config contracts, versioning, migrations, joined state, or source-file creation policy. Hosts can use it for simple render/edit tabs over separate files; any future contract/migration layer must be an explicit opt-in outside this UI metadata work.
close_reason: Completed: added ConfigUiSource metadata and selected_config_source lookup, exported the source API, rendered selected tab source label/path/owner/write mode in the header with legacy active_config_path fallback, kept advanced/unmapped tabs on fallback behavior, documented host-owned source metadata, and added focused model/render tests. Dependency decision unchanged: no new crates; this is UI metadata plumbing over existing PathBuf and owner/read-only fields. Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings, and git diff --check.
